competitor_discovery.why_passed: Bootstrap mode reused an existing competitor set from report-data.json and did not run Tavily in this pass.

recent_news.why_passed: Bootstrap mode reused existing influential-news entries from report-data.json and did not run Tavily in this pass.

reputation_public_web.why_passed: Bootstrap mode reused existing reputation notes from report-data.json and did not run Tavily in this pass.

source_gathering.why_passed: Bootstrap mode reused source-map evidence already present in report-data.json.

notes: Bootstrap summary created from report-data.json rather than live Tavily or Composio calls.
Existing SEO evidence was retained, but this run did not verify SEMrush through direct API or Composio MCP; treat SEMrush as quota-limited/fallback-backed.

composio_mcp.role: Backup provider after direct SEMrush API is unavailable, quota-limited, or blocked.
composio_mcp.note: Use direct Composio MCP tools only. Do not route SEMrush work through Rube when the user has requested Composio, not Rube.

fallback.role: Use only to support SEO/source context when SEMrush direct API and Composio MCP are unavailable or quota-limited.

requests.why: Primary evidence for keyword demand, ranking gaps, and SEO opportunity sizing.
Validates the competitor set before structure and recommendations are locked.
Checks index footprint and helps catch missing or underperforming site sections.
Adds authority context if keyword or competitor evidence is thin.

status_guidance.passed: Use when at least two compact SEMrush-backed proof points can be included.
status_guidance.partial: Use when one SEMrush dataset exists but needs public-web supplementation.
status_guidance.quota_limited: Use when Composio SEMrush quota or entitlement blocks full retrieval.
status_guidance.blocked: Use when SEMrush cannot be authenticated or reached.

semrush_access.fail_condition: A report must not claim SEMrush-backed evidence unless direct-api or composio-mcp returns at least two verified SEMrush evidence points. Jina/public web is fallback context, not SEMrush-backed evidence.
